content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(2, 7, 1), (1, 3, 5), (0, 3, 6), (0, 7, 7), (4, 5, 7), (5, 9, 10), (3, 5, 12), (2, 5, 18), (4, 6, 18), (4, 7, 19), (8, 9, 19), (6, 8, 23), (1, 5, 24), (2, 3, 24), (0, 4, 27)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 1 exactly at time 24.

First, we will go through each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (2, 7, 1) has node 2 and time 1. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (1, 3, 5) has node 1 and time 5. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 3, 6) has node 0 and time 6. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 7, 7) has node 0 and time 7. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 5, 7) has node 4 and time 7. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 9, 10) has node 5 and time 10. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 5, 12) has node 3 and time 12. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 5, 18) has node 2 and time 18. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 6, 18) has node 4 and time 18. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 7, 19) has node 4 and time 19. -> Ignore.
* Edge (8, 9, 19) has node 8 and time 19. -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 8, 23) has node 6 and time 23. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 5, 24) has node 1 and time 24. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (2, 3, 24) has node 2 and time 24. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (0, 4, 27) has node 0 and time 27. -> Ignore.

Now we have collected the following nodes: 7, 5, and 3.

Answer: [7, 5, 3]
